sequence.action: global $wpdb is declared and require_wp_db() is called during bootstrap
sequence.invariant: distribution must keep this call path PHP-visible before most plugin/theme code observes $wpdb
class wpdb must remain loadable even when a db.php drop-in supplies the global object
drop-ins can set global $wpdb to a replacement class before core instantiates wpdb

sequence.action: require_wp_db() returns early when $wpdb is already set
sequence.invariant: Haxe distribution must not eagerly overwrite a drop-in replacement

sequence.action: require_wp_db() instantiates new wpdb(DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, DB_HOST)
sequence.invariant: default construction remains the core-owned fallback when no db.php replacement exists
public field_types/table-prefix mutation must stay compatible with direct property assignment

admin_db_dropin_checks.text: 'db.php'                  => array( __( 'Custom database class.' ), true ),          // Auto on load.

selected_next_slice.selected_because: WPHX-305.21 moved row materialization into typed Haxe, but the next risk is not another SQL branch: it is preserving the wpdb class as a PHP ABI object while Haxe starts owning state defaults and mutation policy. The minimal safe implementation slice is a typed Haxe descriptor plus PHP shell adapter proof, leaving require_wp_db()/db.php replacement timing untouched.
must_preserve: class name wpdb and global $wpdb bootstrap semantics
declared public property names and direct assignment/read behavior
__get/__set/__isset/__unset magic access for protected/private backward compatibility
db.php drop-in early-return behavior in require_wp_db()
wp_set_wpdb_vars() direct field_types assignment and set_prefix() call
WPHX-305.21 row materialization and WPHX-305.20 mysqli @:phpGlobal execution gates
implementation_sketch: Add typed Haxe WpdbPublicStateDescriptor constants for declared public properties, magic-visible internals, default-value policy, and protected write-block names.
Generate a PHP-visible wpdb shell check that verifies declared public properties, AllowDynamicProperties, and magic methods remain reflection-compatible with WordPress 7.0.
Keep constructor, require_wp_db(), db.php inclusion, and global $wpdb replacement in PHP-visible shell code until the descriptor proof passes under bootstrap/drop-in probes.
Only after that proof should individual state fields move to Haxe-owned storage helpers behind the shell.

not_selected_yet.option: replace require_wp_db() with generated Haxe bootstrap
not_selected_yet.reason: Too much load-order and drop-in risk before a property/magic/dynamic contract receipt exists.

not_selected_yet.option: move all wpdb properties into typed Haxe storage immediately
not_selected_yet.reason: Plugins can read/write declared public properties and add dynamic ones; moving storage first would risk reflection and mutation regressions.

not_selected_yet.option: custom PHP target for full wpdb class emission
not_selected_yet.reason: May still become useful, but WPHX-305.22 shows the next blocker is ABI contract proof, not target lowering.

closes_gaps_from.resolution: WPHX-305.22 proves the next ownership boundary as a design receipt: first add a typed public-state descriptor and PHP shell contract proof, preserving db.php replacement timing and PHP-visible wpdb properties before moving individual state storage into Haxe.

remaining_gaps.detail: This receipt selects the next implementation slice but does not yet add WpdbPublicStateDescriptor or a reflection/drop-in shell candidate.
require_wp_db(), db.php inclusion, and global $wpdb replacement remain PHP-visible WordPress shell behavior until the public-state descriptor proof is green.
Live database gates cover the current wpdb slices, but full upstream wpdb/dbDelta/option PHPUnit parity remains a domain closure requirement.
